summary: Machine policy for security questions on local accounts. Microsoft documents the same device policy path and value name that the app writes, and the policy description matches the blocked behavior.

setting.name: Security Questions for Local Accounts
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can let local-account users create security questions so they can reset their password later. This policy decides whether those security questions are allowed.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable security questions on shared, managed, or security-sensitive devices because security questions are a weak recovery method and can be guessed or socially engineered.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off removes a weak password recovery path, but it also makes self-service password reset harder for local-account users.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents this policy through ADMX_CredUI and the LocalPolicySecurityQuestionsSetting CSP. The policy maps to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions. Microsoft documents values 0 and 1, while the local ADML help text explains the enabled behavior.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The machine policy is not forcing security questions off.

allowed_values.label: Security questions allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and local users can use security questions if the feature is otherwise available.

allowed_values.label: Security questions blocked
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and local users cannot set up or use security questions to reset their passwords.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions = 1, which matches the documented blocked state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 RS6 and newer systems that support the local security-questions policy

states.rationale: The Group Policy path is normally not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents the CSP default as 0, while the local ADMX and ADML files describe the policy behavior when it is enabled or left unforced. Treat the unset Group Policy state as the Windows baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that still rely on local-account self-service recovery
avoid_for: Managed devices with stricter identity rules
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to keep the normal local-account recovery option available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You do not want password-reset questions to exist on the device.
tradeoffs: Local-account recovery remains easier for non-technical users.
Security questions stay available unless another control blocks them.

recommended_profiles.label: No security questions
avoid_for: Casual users who depend on security-question recovery

states.rationale: Block local-account security questions through policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to remove a weak password recovery mechanism from local accounts.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: Users may need an easy self-service recovery path for local accounts.
tradeoffs: Security questions cannot be set up or used.
Password recovery for local accounts becomes less convenient.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: ADMX_CredUI / NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the registry path, value name, allowed values 0 and 1, and default behavior for the local security-questions policy.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CredUI.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CredUI.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents local users from setting up and using security questions to reset their passwords.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Microsoft documents NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions directly in the ADMX_CredUI Policy CSP and maps it to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions. The same page describes that enabling the policy prevents local users from setting up and using security questions to reset passwords, and the app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: If you turn this policy setting on, local users won't be able to set up and use security questions to reset their passwords. ADMX mapping: Name NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions ... Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System ... Registry Value Name NoLocalPasswordResetQuestions.
validation_proof.notes: The ADMX_CredUI Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact machine policy path and value name the app writes, and its policy description matches the blocked behavior.
